name: Code Quality with Clippy and rustfmt
description: Enforce code quality and consistent formatting using clippy for linting and rustfmt for automatic code formatting.
trendingReason: Clippy and rustfmt are required in most Rust CI pipelines

steps.title: Run clippy for lint warnings
steps.description: Analyze your code with clippy to catch common mistakes and unidiomatic patterns.
steps.tip: Use -W clippy::pedantic for stricter checks. Use -D warnings to treat warnings as errors in CI.

steps.title: Auto-fix clippy suggestions
steps.description: Apply automatic fixes for issues clippy can resolve.
steps.warning: Review the changes after auto-fixing. Some fixes may change behavior in subtle ways.

steps.title: Format code with rustfmt
steps.description: Apply consistent formatting across the entire project.

steps.title: Check formatting without modifying files
steps.description: Verify that all code is properly formatted, failing if changes are needed. Ideal for CI.

steps.title: Create a rustfmt configuration
steps.description: Customize formatting rules with a rustfmt.toml file.
steps.command:
cat > rustfmt.toml << 'EOF'
max_width = 100
tab_spaces = 4
edition = "2021"
use_field_init_shorthand = true
EOF

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Rust Code Quality: Clippy + rustfmt
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Formatting Check ==="
cargo fmt -- --check && echo "Formatting OK" || { echo "Formatting issues found. Run 'cargo fmt' to fix."; }

echo ""
echo "=== Clippy Lint ==="
cargo clippy -- -D warnings 2>&1

echo ""
echo "=== Quality Commands ==="
echo "Lint:        cargo clippy"
echo "Strict:      cargo clippy -- -W clippy::pedantic"
echo "CI mode:     cargo clippy -- -D warnings"
echo "Auto-fix:    cargo clippy --fix --allow-dirty"
echo "Format:      cargo fmt"
echo "Format check: cargo fmt -- --check"

faq.question: How do I suppress a clippy warning for a specific line?
faq.answer: Add #[allow(clippy::lint_name)] above the item. For a single expression, use #[allow(clippy::lint_name)] on the enclosing block. Prefer fixing the issue over suppressing.

faq.question: What is the difference between clippy::pedantic and default lints?
faq.answer: Default clippy lints catch clear mistakes. Pedantic lints enforce idiomatic Rust style more strictly, including things like missing docs and verbose patterns. Enable pedantic gradually.

faq.question: Can I use rustfmt with nightly-only options?
faq.answer: Yes. Install the nightly toolchain with 'rustup install nightly' and run 'cargo +nightly fmt'. Some options like imports_granularity require nightly.
